request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.raw_input: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.
hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures

references.title: Trehalose-assisted cryopreservation in mammalian cell workflows (seed example)
references.note: Deterministic placeholder reference for demo only.

references.title: Protocol comparison: DMSO baseline vs sugar additives (seed example)
references.note: Deterministic placeholder reference for demo only.

literature_qc.explanation: Similar methods likely exist, but exact intervention/control parameters appear variable.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Use standard DMSO backbone and run a controlled trehalose comparison with explicit assumptions.

protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: Retain baseline cooling/thaw handling while swapping sucrose condition with trehalose arm.
limitations: Seed protocol only, not a verified proprietary SOP.

protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: Run pilot concentration sweep to reduce false negatives from poor trehalose dosing.
limitations: Designed as pilot backbone, not final full-scale method.

evidence_claims.claim: Post-thaw viability directly tests the stated biological outcome.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Outcome is explicitly present in user hypothesis.

evidence_claims.claim: Trehalose membrane stabilization rationale supports intervention plausibility.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Mechanistic rationale is explicitly stated in user input.

evidence_claims.claim: Expected +15 percentage-point threshold may be optimistic without pilot calibration.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Effect size is user-defined but not validated in this run.

evidence_claims.claim: Related work exists but exact comparators and setup differ.
evidence_claims.support_summary: Literature QC returns similar_work_exists with placeholder references.

risks.description: Trehalose intracellular protection may be insufficient in the chosen HeLa setup.
risks.mitigation: Add concentration pilot arm and viability check at 0h and 24h.

risks.description: Comparator alone may be insufficient to explain handling artifacts.
risks.mitigation: Add no-cryoprotectant stress control and sham-freeze control.

risks.description: Single run can overfit to batch effects.
risks.mitigation: Require minimum three biological replicates before execution claim.

risks.description: Cell line provenance and handling approvals must be verified by lab lead.
risks.mitigation: Document institutional biosafety verification before wet-lab execution.

plan.objective: Assess whether trehalose substitution increases post-thaw HeLa viability versus standard DMSO protocol.
plan.experimental_design: Controlled comparative cryopreservation with pilot-first gating and replicate requirement.

step_by_step_protocol.description: Standardize HeLa culture conditions before freezing.
Prepare baseline DMSO and trehalose cryomedia arms.
Freeze with controlled-rate process and log batch metadata.
Mitigation step for RISK-001: Add concentration pilot arm and viability check at 0h and 24h.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add no-cryoprotectant stress control and sham-freeze control.
Measure delayed viability at 24h to detect latent post-thaw damage.

assumptions: Cell handling and thaw timing are standardized across arms.
Cryomedia preparation follows local SOP with documented version.
decision_criteria: Proceed if trehalose arm improves viability by threshold and no contradictory safety flags appear.
Request scientist review before execution approval.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: Add concentration pilot arm and viability check at 0h and 24h.
RISK-002: Add no-cryoprotectant stress control and sham-freeze control.
RISK-003: downgraded to pilot (Require minimum three biological replicates before execution claim.)
reproducibility_notes: Record operator, passage number, reagent lot, freeze curve, and thaw timestamps per batch.
Maintain identical viability assay window and plate layout across comparator and intervention arms.

materials.uncertainty_note: Exact catalog and lot to be confirmed by lab procurement.
Catalog varies by region and purity grade.
Pricing and grade are approximate pending concentration protocol.

line_items.uncertainty_note: Low confidence until exact grade is fixed.
Assay kit pricing varies by vendor and format.

uncertainty_notes: All costs are deterministic demo estimates.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: 15 percentage points improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Assess whether trehalose substitution increases post-thaw HeLa viability versus standard DMSO protocol.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
